scenes.trigger: Right now, somewhere in the world
scenes.text: Right now, somewhere in the world, a person is asleep in a tent.

scenes.trigger: Completely unconscious
scenes.text: Completely unconscious. Zero awareness.

scenes.trigger: a thin piece of nylon
scenes.text: A thin piece of nylon between them and a forest full of animals that could end them in seconds.

scenes.trigger: Lions. Wolves. Leopards
scenes.text: Lions. Wolves. Leopards.

scenes.trigger: smell you from a mile away
scenes.text: Animals that can smell you from a mile away and see perfectly in pitch black darkness.

scenes.trigger: almost every single time, that person wakes up
scenes.text: And yet, almost every single time, that person wakes up in the morning. Why?

scenes.trigger: Why?
A sleeping human is just meat
scenes.text: A sleeping human is just meat.

content.quote:
A sleeping human
is just meat.

scenes.trigger: Quiet meat
scenes.text: Slow meat. Quiet meat. Meat that cannot run and cannot fight back.

scenes.trigger: all you can eat buffet
scenes.text: You would think predators would treat campsites like an all you can eat buffet.

scenes.trigger: They do not
scenes.text: They do not. And the reason is not one simple answer. It is several layered reasons stacked on top of each other, going back further than civilization itself. Let's start with something that sounds like an ego trip but is actually just biology.

scenes.trigger: You are terrifying
scenes.text: You are terrifying.

scenes.trigger: kind of a disaster as an animal
scenes.text: Not you personally. Humans, as a species, are genuinely one of the most feared things in the natural world.

scenes.trigger: In 2019, researchers at UC Santa Cruz
scenes.text: In 2019, researchers at UC Santa Cruz

scenes.trigger: played recordings of human voices near mountain lions
scenes.text: played recordings of human voices near mountain lions in California.

scenes.trigger: The mountain lions fled
scenes.text: The mountain lions fled. At the same rate they fled from barking dogs.

scenes.trigger: sometimes faster
scenes.text: Sometimes faster.

scenes.trigger: dragging a full grown deer up a hillside
scenes.text: These are animals capable of dragging a full grown deer up a hillside.

scenes.trigger: absolute units of predatory efficiency
They heard a human voice and ran
scenes.text: They heard a human voice and ran.

scenes.trigger: They heard a podcast and ran
Mountain lions abandoned food they had already killed
scenes.text: The same study found something even more telling. Mountain lions abandoned food they had already killed. Food they were actively eating. The moment they heard a human voice nearby, they walked away from a meal

scenes.trigger: walked away from a meal they had already paid for
scenes.text: they had already paid for in calories and risk. This pattern repeats everywhere.

scenes.trigger: Lions in Africa avoid areas with human activity
scenes.text: Lions in Africa avoid areas with human activity.

scenes.trigger: Tigers in India give entire villages a wide berth
scenes.text: Tigers in India give entire villages a wide berth.

scenes.trigger: Jaguars in the Amazon have adjusted their movement
scenes.text: Jaguars in the Amazon have adjusted their movement to stay away from us. These are not animals that are physically incapable of attacking. These are animals that have

scenes.trigger: decided, over thousands of generations, that we are not worth the risk
scenes.text: decided, over thousands of generations, that we are not worth the risk. Here is the brutal reason why.

scenes.trigger: Humans have been hunting large predators for around 2 million years
scenes.text: Humans have been hunting large predators for around 2 million years.

scenes.trigger: We wiped out mammoths
scenes.text: We wiped out mammoths.

scenes.trigger: We eliminated saber toothed cats
scenes.text: We eliminated saber toothed cats.

scenes.trigger: wiped megafauna off entire continents
We hunted wolves to near extinction
scenes.text: We hunted wolves to near extinction across entire continents.

scenes.trigger: eliminated mountain lions from the entire eastern half
The predators that survived were the ones that learned to fear us
scenes.text: The predators that survived were the ones that learned to fear us. The bold ones, the ones that approached human camps, got killed. The cautious ones lived and

scenes.trigger: passed that caution to their offspring
scenes.text: passed that caution to their offspring. Over thousands of years,

scenes.trigger: that learned caution became instinct
scenes.text: that learned caution became instinct.

scenes.trigger: It became biological memory
scenes.text: It became biological memory. So when a predator picks up a human scent at night,

scenes.trigger: an ancient alarm goes off
scenes.text: an ancient alarm goes off somewhere deep in its nervous system. Not a conscious thought. A wired in signal passed down from every ancestor that survived an encounter with us. Now let's talk about smell.

scenes.trigger: you probably never think about how you smell
scenes.text: You probably never think about how you smell, because you are surrounded by other humans constantly. But to a predator with a sense of smell that can be

scenes.trigger: a hundred thousand times more sensitive than yours
scenes.text: a hundred thousand times more sensitive than yours,

scenes.trigger: Jacobson's organ
you are receiving a flood of olfactory data
scenes.text: you are

scenes.trigger: broadcasting an enormous and deeply strange chemical signal
scenes.text: broadcasting an enormous and deeply strange chemical signal.

scenes.trigger: You smell like synthetic fabric
scenes.text: You smell like synthetic fabric.

scenes.trigger: Processed food
scenes.text: Processed food.

scenes.trigger: Soap, shampoo, sunscreen, insect repellent
scenes.text: Soap, shampoo, sunscreen, insect repellent. A cocktail of artificial compounds with

scenes.trigger: no equivalent anywhere in the natural world
scenes.text: no equivalent anywhere in the natural world.

scenes.trigger: you smell like a primate
scenes.text: A predator's hunting instinct is

scenes.trigger: calibrated to specific prey it has hunted thousands of times
scenes.text: calibrated to specific prey it has hunted thousands of times.

scenes.trigger: You do not fit that pattern. You are an anomaly
scenes.text: You do not fit that pattern. You are an anomaly. And

scenes.trigger: anomalies trigger caution, not aggression
scenes.text: anomalies trigger caution, not aggression. Then there is fire.

scenes.trigger: fear of fire is one of the only nearly universal responses
scenes.text: Fear of fire is one of the only nearly universal responses across the entire animal kingdom.

scenes.trigger: Animals that were afraid of fire in the wild survived wildfires
It is not learned. It is innate
scenes.text: It is not learned. It is innate. Animals raised in laboratories who have never seen fire still

scenes.trigger: show avoidance behavior at the smell of smoke
scenes.text: show fear at the smell of smoke.

scenes.trigger: Humans discovered fire somewhere between one and two million years ago
scenes.text: Humans discovered fire somewhere between one and two million years ago, and we have been

scenes.trigger: sleeping next to it ever since
scenes.text: sleeping next to it ever since. A campfire does not just provide warmth. It

scenes.trigger: broadcasts a signal into the darkness
scenes.text: broadcasts a signal into the darkness that says

scenes.trigger: something dangerous controls this flame. Stay away
scenes.text: something dangerous controls this flame. Stay away. That message has been received for so long, predators do not even need to think about it anymore.

content.quote:
“Something dangerous
controls this flame.
Stay away.”

scenes.trigger: It triggers automatic avoidance
scenes.text: It triggers automatic avoidance. Now let's destroy a myth that movies have been selling for decades.

scenes.trigger: Predators are not reckless
scenes.text: Predators are not reckless. In reality,

scenes.trigger: every decision a predator makes is a survival calculation
scenes.text: every decision a predator makes is a survival calculation. A lion does not have health insurance.

scenes.trigger: A broken leg or a deep wound can mean a slow death
scenes.text: A broken leg or a deep wound can mean a slow death by starvation.

scenes.trigger: One serious injury... can mean a slow death by starvation
scenes.text: A sleeping human fails this calculation in multiple ways. First, unfamiliarity.

scenes.trigger: A predator knows exactly how a zebra runs
scenes.text: A predator knows exactly how a zebra runs and where its blind spots are.

scenes.trigger: It has no idea how a human will respond
scenes.text: It has no idea how a human will respond. We might wake up screaming.

scenes.trigger: We might have tools nearby
scenes.text: We might have weapons. We might have other humans nearby. That uncertainty alone is a massive red flag.

scenes.trigger: Is the caloric value of this target worth the risk of injury
scenes.text: Second, our size.

scenes.trigger: humans standing upright look larger and more threatening
scenes.text: Humans standing upright look larger and more threatening than our actual body weight suggests. Researchers have noted that standing bipedal is itself a kind of threat display,

scenes.trigger: similar to what gorillas and bears do when trying to look intimidating
scenes.text: similar to what gorillas and bears do when trying to look intimidating. Third, and most simply, why bother.

scenes.trigger: There is almost always an easier meal nearby
scenes.text: There is almost always an easier meal nearby. Going after something strange and unpredictable when a familiar deer is right there

scenes.trigger: makes no survival sense at all
scenes.text: makes no survival sense at all. Here is something that almost never comes up.

scenes.trigger: Your sleep is not as deep as you think
scenes.text: Your sleep is not as deep as you think.

content.quote:
Your sleep is not
as deep as you think.

scenes.trigger: Your brain cycles through different sleep stages
scenes.text: Your brain cycles through different sleep stages, and during the lighter stages, it remains surprisingly responsive to the environment. Scientists call this

scenes.trigger: the sentinel function of sleep
scenes.text: the sentinel function of sleep. Even while resting, your brain keeps a partial watch,

scenes.trigger: flagging unfamiliar sounds and unusual changes
scenes.text: flagging unfamiliar sounds and unusual changes in the environment. The humans who slept too deeply, who failed to wake for nighttime threats,

scenes.trigger: did not survive to pass on their genes
scenes.text: did not survive to pass on their genes.

scenes.trigger: The light sleepers, the ones who could go from asleep to on their feet in seconds, were the ones who survived
scenes.text: The light sleepers, the ones who could go from asleep to on their feet in seconds, were the ones who survived. Over hundreds of thousands of years,

scenes.trigger: human sleep got tuned to stay just alert enough
scenes.text: human sleep got tuned to stay just alert enough to respond to danger.

scenes.trigger: we sleep lighter and in shorter cycles than most large mammals
scenes.text: And then there is the group factor.

scenes.trigger: Humans rarely camp alone
scenes.text: Humans rarely camp alone. Predators understand group dynamics deeply, because

scenes.trigger: their prey animals also form groups
scenes.text: their prey animals also form groups.

scenes.trigger: Predators don't just attack prey, they hunt for separation
scenes.text: A predator looks for separation. The isolated individual. The one cut off from the herd.

scenes.trigger: A wildebeest in the middle of a herd is much safer
A fish in the center of a school is much harder to target
A camp full of sleeping humans is a group
scenes.text: A camp full of sleeping humans is a group. And a group that wakes up together, responds together, and defends together is exactly

scenes.trigger: a chaotic, high risk scenario predators are evolved to avoid
scenes.text: the kind of chaotic, high risk scenario predators are evolved to avoid. Now, to be honest,

scenes.trigger: none of this means you are untouchable
scenes.text: none of this means you are untouchable.

scenes.trigger: always involve a predator that has broken from the normal rules
scenes.text: The attacks that do happen follow a consistent pattern. They almost always involve a predator that has broken from the normal rules.

scenes.trigger: Injured. Starving. Or an animal that has lost its inherited fear because humans fed it
scenes.text: Injured. Starving. Or an animal that has lost its inherited fear because humans fed it and taught it we are a source of food, not danger.

scenes.trigger: The Tsavo lions that killed dozens of railway workers in 1898
scenes.text: The Tsavo lions that killed dozens of railway workers in 1898 were not typical lions.

scenes.trigger: One had severe dental damage
scenes.text: One had severe dental damage that made normal hunting nearly impossible.

scenes.trigger: The Champawat tiger... shot in the mouth years earlier
scenes.text: The Champawat tiger, responsible for hundreds of deaths in the early 1900s, had been shot in the mouth years earlier, shattering its canine teeth.

scenes.trigger: the legend grew in the retelling
habitat encroachment
scenes.text: These were not examples of normal predator behavior. They were

scenes.trigger: exceptions created by exceptional damage
scenes.text: exceptions created by exceptional damage.

scenes.trigger: the rule holds almost universally
scenes.text: Here is the most mind bending part of this entire story. We have become so feared by the natural world that

scenes.trigger: our presence now reshapes entire ecosystems
scenes.text: our presence now reshapes entire ecosystems without us doing anything at all. When predators avoid areas with human activity,

scenes.trigger: prey populations in those areas grow unchecked
scenes.text: prey populations in those areas grow unchecked.

scenes.trigger: medium-sized predators, coyotes, bobcats, foxes, also reduce their presence
rodent populations they normally control start expanding
That changes plant life. That changes smaller predator populations
scenes.text: That changes plant life. That changes smaller predator populations. That changes everything down the food chain. One species. One inherited fear.

scenes.trigger: Rippling through the entire living world
scenes.text: Rippling through the entire living world.

scenes.trigger: The most dangerous animal on the planet figured out fire
scenes.text: The most dangerous animal on the planet figured out fire before it figured out almost anything else.

content.quote:
The most dangerous animal
figured out fire first.

scenes.trigger: It sleeps light. It travels in groups
scenes.text: It sleeps light. It travels in groups. And it carries

scenes.trigger: thousands of generations of successful survival
scenes.text: thousands of generations of successful survival written into its biology.

scenes.trigger: That is you
scenes.text: That is you. So the next time you are

scenes.trigger: lying in a tent, listening to the sounds of the dark
scenes.text: lying in a tent, listening to the sounds of the dark beyond a thin nylon wall, and you feel that flicker of ancient anxiety.

scenes.trigger: that feeling kept your ancestors alive
scenes.text: That feeling kept your ancestors alive. But the thing outside the tent.

content.quote:
That feeling kept
your ancestors alive.

scenes.trigger: it already knows better than to come in
scenes.text: It already knows better than to come in.

scenes.trigger: END CARD

cta.big:
THE THING OUTSIDE
ALREADY KNOWS BETTER.
